title: spermacoce malabarica
intro: Spermacoce malabarica, also known as Malabar bugleweed, is a plant species that belongs to the Rubiaceae family. It is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory problems, and aiding in the treatment of digestive disorders. Spermacoce malabarica is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions for its medicinal purposes.
